title: rosa × hibernica f. laevigata
intro: Rosa × hibernica f. laevigata, also known as the Irish rose, is a fragrant, evergreen climbing rose that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is a hybrid of Rosa rubiginosa and Rosa acicularis. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rosa × hibernica f. laevigata include its ability to soothe and calm the mind and body, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Rosa × hibernica f. laevigata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
